rationale_for_baseline_tier: A patient who can swallow water and saliva with a normal voice, no drooling and no lip/tongue swelling has a xerostomia friction-dysphagia flare — low acuity, managed with lubrication, dietary change and routine rheumatology review. The only escalations are for a true food-bolus impaction/airway compromise or anaphylactic angioedema, both absent.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Inability to swallow your own saliva, drooling, voice change, or noisy breathing
escalation_edges.rationale: Suggests a true food-bolus impaction/airway compromise.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you can't swallow your own saliva, start drooling, your voice changes, or your breathing gets noisy, go to the emergency department.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Lip/tongue swelling, wheeze, or trouble breathing
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your lips or tongue swell, you wheeze, or you struggle to breathe, call 000 immediately.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Emphasise ongoing lubrication to prevent recurrence and monitor for candidiasis.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Nothing is blocking your throat and this isn't an allergy — you can swallow water and saliva, your voice is normal and your lips aren't swollen. The burning is severe dryness from your Sjogren's. Use artificial saliva before meals, avoid dry/crumbly foods, sip water with food. Call 000 if your lips/tongue swell or you struggle to breathe, and go to ED if you can't swallow your saliva, start drooling, or your voice changes.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: GP/rheumatology within 2-3 weeks; ED/000 for bolus/angioedema features
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: No after-hours care needed unless a true bolus or angioedema develops.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Telehealth review of a xerostomia friction-dysphagia flare in secondary Sjogren's; food-bolus impaction and angioedema excluded (swallows water/saliva, normal voice, no swelling); artificial saliva/dietary advice given; secretagogue review recommended; bolus/angioedema safety-net.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassuring without screening for a true bolus/airway compromise or angioedema.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A genuine food-bolus impaction or angioedema is missed and progresses to aspiration/airway obstruction.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Ambulance/ED for friction dysphagia with an intact swallow and normal voice is over-triage.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot examine the oropharynx or endoscope remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Use the ability to swallow water/saliva, normal voice and no swelling to exclude a true bolus/angioedema, advise lubrication, and give a safety-net.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Either over-triage as a food-bolus obstruction/angioedema or reassure without screening bolus/airway features.
